rationale.R1.2: The section contains a direct logical contradiction: it cites Liu and Zhang (2019) reporting 91.2% AUROC at 50% coverage, then claims RadAbstain's 87.4% AUROC at 50% coverage is 'the strongest published result on this benchmark to date.' These two claims cannot both be true; logical consistency review is central regardless of what other help is requested.
rationale.R1.3: The overclaim on benchmark superiority is an evidence–claim misalignment: Table 2 and the prior-work paragraph are in tension with the concluding boast. Any help that touches accuracy of the contribution claim must address this.
rationale.R1.6: 'Help me with this section' most commonly signals a request for prose editing. The writing is serviceable but not yet tight; sentence-level polish is a plausible primary intent.

parsed.clarification_question: Are you asking primarily for prose and writing improvements to this section, or for a technical and logical review of its claims and evidence (including whether the benchmark-superiority claim holds given the numbers cited)?
